    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",

    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "The draft claims a 'multi-agent collaborative framework' but describes a standard single-network architecture where gradients are simply summed. Should the improvement focus on (A) rewriting the technical description to match a true multi-agent architecture, or (B) removing the 'multi-agent' framing to accurately describe the single-network method presented?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The core 'multi-agent' concept is contradicted by the technical description of a single network with a summed-gradient update.",
    "R1.2": "The logical chain from the problem (gradient conflict) to the solution (multi-agent collaboration) is broken by the described implementation, which appears to be the standard monolithic approach.",
    "R1.3": "The central claim of a multi-agent mechanism is not supported by the evidence provided in the architectural and training description.",
    "R1.6": "The general request to 'improve' the draft makes sentence-level clarity, flow, and conciseness a central responsibility.",
    "RX.1": "The agent must disclose its uncertainty about whether the core concept or its technical description is the intended one.",
    "RX.2": "The draft currently overclaims a multi-agent contribution, and any improvement must rectify this potential misrepresentation.",
    "RX.3": "The agent must adhere to the scope of improving the text, not inventing a new technical method to fit the narrative.",
    "RX.4": "Correcting the potential misrepresentation of the method is necessary to avoid misleading the scientific community.",
    "RX.5": "All suggested improvements must be traceable to the identified inconsistencies between the draft's claims and its description."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: The draft claims a 'multi-agent collaborative framework' but describes a standard single-network architecture where gradients are simply summed. Should the improvement focus on (A) rewriting the technical description to match a true multi-agent architecture, or (B) removing the 'multi-agent' framing to accurately describe the single-network method presented?

rationale.R1.1: The core 'multi-agent' concept is contradicted by the technical description of a single network with a summed-gradient update.
rationale.R1.2: The logical chain from the problem (gradient conflict) to the solution (multi-agent collaboration) is broken by the described implementation, which appears to be the standard monolithic approach.
rationale.R1.3: The central claim of a multi-agent mechanism is not supported by the evidence provided in the architectural and training description.
rationale.R1.6: The general request to 'improve' the draft makes sentence-level clarity, flow, and conciseness a central responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: The agent must disclose its uncertainty about whether the core concept or its technical description is the intended one.
rationale.RX.2: The draft currently overclaims a multi-agent contribution, and any improvement must rectify this potential misrepresentation.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must adhere to the scope of improving the text, not inventing a new technical method to fit the narrative.
rationale.RX.4: Correcting the potential misrepresentation of the method is necessary to avoid misleading the scientific community.
rationale.RX.5: All suggested improvements must be traceable to the identified inconsistencies between the draft's claims and its description.
